sections.paragraph:
ἐπιπίπτω [ἐπί, πίπτω] ‘come upon with sudden movement’, fall upon

—a. in display of special interest or concern Mk 3:10 (crowd pressure); Ac 20:10; ἐ. ἐπὶ τ. τράχηλον αὐτοῦ threw his arms around his neck Lk 15:20. Cp. the gesture of the ‘beloved disciple’ J 13:25 v.l.

—b. in imagery of extraordinary events, states, or conditions Ac 8:16; 10:44; 19:17; 23:7 v.l. (ἐπέπεσεν στάσις a dispute broke out); Ro 15:3; Rv 11:11.
